steps.title: Identify the subject and the grammar rule
steps.content: The subject is Objects — plural. The long appositive phrase "such as 2010 BL4, a 16-kilometer-wide body first detected in 2010" is parenthetical and does not change the subject. Rule: a plural subject requires the base form of the verb (no -s/-es ending).

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — exhibits: Third-person singular present tense; the -s ending marks singular agreement, which disagrees with plural "Objects." ✗
  • Choice B — exhibit: Base-form plural present tense; agrees with plural "Objects." ✓
  • Choice C — is exhibiting: Singular present-progressive ("is" is singular); disagrees with the plural subject. ✗
  • Choice D — has exhibited: Singular present-perfect ("has" is singular); disagrees with the plural subject. ✗
That is the correct answer.
